reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with high fidelity to the text 'Don\'t wake him up, he worked all night.' (00:00 - 00:04). The core requirement to read with a 'quietly volume' is perfectly executed. The model adopts a distinct whispering style throughout the entire clip, which is the most effective way to convey 'quietly' in this context (avoiding waking someone up). The pacing is slow and deliberate, enhancing the naturalness of the whisper. There are no content errors or artifacts. The paralinguistic compliance is spot-on.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the text with a standard, conversational volume that completely ignores the instruction 'with a quietly volume'. Throughout the clip (00:00 - 00:04), the voice is projected clearly and loudly, which contradicts the context of 'Don\'t wake him up'. Furthermore, regarding content accuracy, there appears to be a minor mispronunciation or addition at the end of the sentence, sounding like 'nights' instead of 'night'. Because the core controllable feature (Volume: Quiet) is completely absent—and arguably the opposite (standard/loud volume) is presented—it fails the paralinguistic criteria entirely.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed the instructions perfectly. It recognized that 'quietly volume' in the context of 'Don\'t wake him up' implies a whisper. The resulting audio is contextually appropriate, correctly paced, and adheres strictly to the volume constraint. Model 2 failed to adjust the volume. It delivered the line in a standard, full-voice tone that would likely wake the person up, directly violating the prompt's intent. Additionally, Model 2 had a minor content issue with the word 'night'. Therefore, Model 1 is the clear winner due to its accurate rendering of the paralinguistic feature.
